items.title: ECB chief economist sees persistent impact on inflation from Iran war
items.summary: TOKYO: The energy shock caused by the Middle East conflict will likely have a persistent impact on inflation even if there is a quick solution to the war, the European Central Bank's (ECB) chief economist, Philip Lane, said on Thursday. "Even if the initial energy shock starts to reverse, the second round (effect) will be with us for a while," he said in a conference hosted by the BOJ and its think tank in Tokyo. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Death toll from US chemical spill expected to hit 11
items.summary: LOS ANGELES, (United States): A chemical incident at a paper plant in the northwestern US has killed two people, with nine others missing and feared dead, officials said Wednesday. Two people have been confirmed dead, and efforts to rescue the nine unaccounted for have now been abandoned, Scott Goldstein of Cowlitz 2 Fire and Rescue said. Nine other people were injured.

items.title: UK vehicle production dips in April amid global market strains
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: UK auto production fell 1.2 per cent year-on-year in April as a drop in commercial vehicle output and weak exports to China weighed on the industry, data published by the Society of Motor Manufacturers and Traders (SMMT) on Thursday showed. Total UK vehicle production fell to 58,513 units in April, according to the trade body, with car output broadly stable for a second consecutive month and commercial vehicle volumes declining. April car exports to the European Union rose 6.9 perr cent year-on-year to 23,103 units, shipments to the United States increased 6.8 per cent, while exports to China slumped 44.4 per cent.

items.title: Sime Darby faces challenging outlook despite Q3 profit surge, says PublicInvest
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sime Darby Bhd expects a challenging business environment ahead amid persistent geopolitical tensions and rising inflationary pressures, according to a note by Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest). The research house said operating conditions are likely to remain tough across the group's key markets, particularly in China, which remains in a consolidation phase marked by intense domestic price competition. Meanwhile, PublicInvest said Sime Darby's third quarter of financial year 2026 (Q3 FY26) headline net profit surged more than three-fold year-on-year (YoY) to RM654.0 million, mainly driven by a one-off gain of RM434.0 million from the disposal of Malaysia Vision Valley (MVV) land.

items.title: One in six UK young people risk falling outside job market or education
items.summary: LONDON: The proportion of young people in Britain who are not in employment, education or training (NEETs) risks rising to one in six within five years from one in eight currently, according to a new government-commissioned report. For too many young people it is becoming permanent. We are at risk of a lost generation," former health minister Alan Milburn said in remarks ahead of the report's publication on Thursday.

items.title: Bukit Aman to meet former MACC chief Azam Baki next week over Albert Tei threat allegations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Police will meet former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki next week over allegations he threatened businessman Albert Tei. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M Kumar said police had already contacted Azam and would record his statement in the coming days. Yesterday, police said Azam would be called in soon to assist investigations into the alleged threats made against the businessman.

items.title: Day four of search for missing Gunung Batu Putih hiker continues as rescuers remain overnight on mountain
items.summary: IPOH, May 28 — The third day of the search and rescue (SAR) operation for a female hiker who went missing after climbing Gunung Batu Putih near Tapah on Saturday has yet to yield results and will resume today. Perak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) operations assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said 12 members of the SAR team would remain overnight at the search location at the summit tonight after the operation was suspended late yesterday evening. “The SAR team, which entered the operation area this morning via an AW139 aircraft at Bukit Berapit 2, reached the Kem Sukaneka area after a five-hour trek along the mountain ridge.

items.title: King donates 148 cattle for Aidiladha sacrifice across Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 28 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has contributed 148 cattle for the Aidiladha sacrificial rites to be distributed to the people of Johor. Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the contribution reflected the care, compassion and close bond between the Johor Royal Institution and the people, with the welfare of Bangsa Johor always prioritised. Meanwhile, Onn Hafiz, who is also Machap assemblyman, said the Simpang Renggam Korban Perdana programme would also involve the distribution of 32 sacrificial cattle to mosques, surau, villages and those in need in the area.

items.title: Police confirm two more killed in Kuala Krai Alphard-Bezza crash, raising death toll to three
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Two more victims of a crash involving a Toyota Alphard and a Perodua Bezza along Jalan Gua Musang-Kuala Krai this morning have died, including a one-year-old girl. The infant, Nur Nuha Helena Abd Matin, and her mother, 22-year-old Jasmin Lyana Mohd Jamil, died while receiving treatment at Hospital Sultan Ismail Petra (HSIP), police said to Berita Harian . Kuala Krai deputy police chief Deputy Superintendent Mohd Maizan Mustaffa said the latest deaths bring the toll from the 7.15am crash to three.

items.title: Death toll in Philippine under-construction building collapse rises to five, 16 still missing
items.summary: MANILA: The death toll from the collapse of a building under construction in Pampanga province, northern Philippines, rose to five, authorities said on Wednesday, reported Xinhua. The Angeles City Information Office said search teams were carefully extricating another body from beneath the rubble after a cadaver dog detected the victim at around 6.18pm. – BERNAMA © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Ringgit opens mostly higher against major currencies, slightly lower versus greenback
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened mostly higher against major currencies today but eased slightly against the US dollar as investors stayed cautious over ongoing United States-Iran negotiations. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid told Bernama uncertainties revolving around war in Iran continue to dominate market sentiment despite major equity bourses reaching a record high, such as the US. Meanwhile, the US Dollar Index (DXY) hovered around 99.21 points, slightly higher by 0.04 per cent.

items.title: PM Anwar praises Pakistan’s role in Iran-US talks, backs peaceful diplomacy on West Asia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Malaysia appreciates Pakistan’s role in the ongoing mediation efforts involving negotiations between Iran and the United States, including the country’s willingness to host the next round of talks. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the matter was discussed during a telephone conversation with his Pakistani counterpart, Shehbaz Sharif, yesterday, adding that they also touched on various regional developments and efforts to strengthen Malaysia-Pakistan bilateral relations. “During the telephone conversation with my Pakistani counterpart, Shehbaz Sharif, we discussed various regional developments and efforts to strengthen the longstanding Malaysia-Pakistan bilateral relations.

items.title: #NSTviral: Car gets stuck in motorcycle toll lane, sparks online backlash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 19-second video showing a blue sedan wedged inside a narrow motorcycle lane beside a toll plaza has gone viral, drawing amusement and criticism from social media users. Another user, Suffian Suffi, wrote: "It probably entered the lane to avoid paying the toll. User Su Haifee also questioned the driver's motive, commenting: "Trying to avoid paying the toll?"

items.title: Man, 67, crushed under own Proton Saga after forgetting to engage handbrake in Batang Kali, police say
items.summary: Hulu Selangor District Police Chief ACP Ibrahim Husin said they received a report regarding the incident involving the 67-year-old victim at 5.30pm. “Initial investigations revealed that the accident occurred at 5.18 pm when the victim parked his vehicle inside his home garage before stepping out to close the gate. Ibrahim said that preliminary findings suggest the victim is suspected to have failed to pull the vehicle’s handbrake, combined with the fact that the garage floor is sloped downwards towards the gate.

items.title: Fed says monetary policy is 'well positioned' amid inflation risks
items.summary: NEW YORK: Federal Reserve vice chair Philip Jefferson said that the current setting of monetary policy is in the right place amid ongoing upside risks to the inflation outlook. Jefferson, the central bank's second-in-command, stopped short of saying what is next for interest rate policy, noting that when it comes to the June 16-17 Federal Open Market Committee meeting. Jefferson expects inflation pressures to wane later this year but said there are upside risks to this outlook.

items.title: Amazon greenlights AI-generated shows for children
items.summary: LOS ANGELES, (United States): Amazon MGM Studios announced Wednesday it has greenlit the first three children's shows that were created under a new initiative to use Artificial Intelligence (AI) in content development. The GenAI Creators Fund pays filmmakers, digital creators and startups to use AI to develop their projects in a short time frame. "Punky Duck" series director Jorge Gutierrez said he is used to spending two years making a pilot, but his new show was greenlit to run on Amazon after just two months.

items.title: Cambodia sentences six for murder of South Korean student linked to scam centre
items.summary: PHNOM PENH: A Cambodian court has convicted and sentenced six Chinese nationals to life in prison on charges they tortured and murdered a South Korean student involved with one of Cambodia's notorious scam centres, a court spokesperson said on Wednesday. The student's death in August last year kicked off a diplomatic firestorm with Seoul, which issued travel bans for parts of Cambodia, imposed sanctions, and launched joint efforts to crack down on the sprawling centres, which have been accused of enslaving and abusing workers and stealing billions of dollars from scam victims around the world. The Kampot Provincial Court found all six men guilty of torture, murder, and aggravated fraud, the spokesperson said in a statement.

items.title: Russia says US did not grant visa for official to attend UN meeting
items.summary: UNITED STATES: Russia's UN ambassador said on Tuesday that the United States did not grant a visa for Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Alexander Alimov to attend a UN Security Council meeting and called it a breach of US obligations as host of the UN. Vassily Nebenzia made the comment at a meeting of the 15-member UN Security Council chaired by China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi that he said Alimov had intended to attend. A UN diplomat said Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi had also apparently been denied a visa to attend the same meeting, but a US State Department official said on Wednesday that Washington did not prevent the minister from travelling to New York.

items.title: Oil rebounds after US strikes Iran military site
items.summary: Brent crude futures rose US$1.90, or 2.02 per cent, to US$96.19 a barrel by 0015 GMT, while the more active August contract gained US$1.64, or 1.78 per cent, to US$93.89. US West Texas Intermediate futures were up US$1.73, or 1.95 per cent, at US$90.41. The US military launched new strikes in Iran targeting a military site that officials believed posed a threat to US forces and commercial maritime traffic in the strait, a US official told Reuters.

items.title: US carries out new strikes in Iran against military site
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US military carried out new strikes overnight in Iran targeting a military site that officials believed posed a threat to US forces and commercial maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, a US official told Reuters on Wednesday. The official, who spoke on condition of anonymity, said the US military has also intercepted and shot down multiple Iranian drones that posed a similar threat. The US targets included boats attempting to lay mines and missile launch sites that the US military's Central Command said posed a threat to US forces.

items.title: Manufacturers, SMEs risk temporary shutdown amid Middle East conflict - FMM
items.summary: FMM president Jacob Lee Chor Kok said the group's second survey on the Middle East impact found that 70 per cent of manufacturers are now experiencing critical shortages of key production inputs such as resin, polymers, petrochemicals and metals. He said about eight per cent of FMM members have less than two weeks of critical stock, while another 40 per cent are holding inventory sufficient for only one to two months. He said the FMM survey confirmed that 72 per cent of manufacturers are experiencing worsening operating conditions, with 74.5 per cent reporting production costs rising by at least 10 per cent compared to pre-crisis levels, while 68 per cent are facing working capital and cash flow challenges due to delayed payments from buyers and tighter supplier payment terms.

items.title: Sabah, Sarawak rail plans to boost construction pipeline, execution key [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's construction sector could see a fresh pipeline of contracts from expanding rail plans in Sabah and Sarawak, potentially boosting contractor workloads and sustaining activity. Industry players said the pipeline could offset slower momentum in some segments by unlocking long-duration infrastructure work, particularly in civil engineering, tunnelling, station development and related industrial facilities. Samuel Tan, founder and chief executive officer of Olive Tree Property Consultants said: "Large-scale rail projects typically provide multi-year visibility for contractors, from earthworks through to systems integration.

items.title: Banks shift battle to decision speed as competition intensifies: Moody's
items.summary: A report by Moody's Corporation said speed of decision-making, alongside traditional credit judgement and risk assessment, is becoming a defining source of competitive advantage in the banking sector as new entrants reshape industry dynamics. More than 80 per cent of banks also report fragmented data systems and legacy infrastructure as a major constraint, underscoring the operational challenges facing incumbents. Artificial intelligence (AI) adoption and governance also feature prominently.

items.title: Early warning signs of financial distress often overlooked by businesses, says BDO Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Companies that fail to spot early warning signs of financial stress risk losing critical turnaround options as financial distress often develops gradually rather than emerging suddenly. BDO Malaysia executive director of advice, Bernard Tan, said companies that identify financial warning signs early are better positioned to preserve strategic flexibility, stabilise operations, and pursue restructuring efforts before value erodes. He said companies often mistake steady revenue growth for financial strength, even as profitability and cash generation begin to weaken.

items.title: Jill Biden feared Joe was 'having a stroke' during 2024 debate
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Former US first lady Jill Biden says in an upcoming interview that her husband's disastrous 2024 debate performance against Donald Trump was so alarming, she thought he was having a stroke on stage. In the debate, then 81-year-old president Joe Biden stumbled on his words, stared open-mouthed and lost his train of thought as he struggled to counter his bombastic rival. Yet immediately after the debate, Jill Biden praised her husband's performance, telling him in front of a crowd of supporters: "Joe, you did such a great job.

items.title: CIMB sees near-term margin pressure for 7-Eleven Malaysia on higher costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: 7-Eleven Malaysia Holdings Bhd's near-term earnings are expected to remain under pressure amid elevated operating expenses due to its ongoing revenue growth initiatives. CIMB Securities Research said 7-Eleven Malaysia's gross profit margin contracted 1.0 percentage point (pp) year-on-year (YoY) to 29.7 per cent from 30.7 per cent in the first quarter (Q1) 2025. 7-Eleven Malaysia's first quarter (Q1) 2026 earnings before interest and tax (EBIT) declined 19.8 per cent year-on-year (YoY), while profit before tax (PBT) fell 41.8 per cent YoY as operating expenses rose 12.5 per cent YoY.

items.title: Malaysia warned against unchecked data centre boom
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia should be more selective in approving future data centre projects as the fast-growing industry places increasing pressure on electricity, water and land resources, industry experts said. The warning comes as Malaysia cements its position as one of Southeast Asia's fastest-growing data centre hubs, drawing billions of dollars in investments from global technology firms and hyperscalers seeking alternatives to land- and power-constrained Singapore. Malaysia has emerged as one of Southeast Asia's fastest-growing data centre markets in recent years, attracting investments from global hyperscalers and technology firms seeking alternatives to Singapore due to land and power constraints there.

items.title: WCE Section 3 completion lifts IJM outlook, says CIMB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The completion of Section 3 of the West Coast Expressway (WCE) is expected to remove a key overhang for IJM Corp Bhd, according to CIMB Securities Research. Section 3 of the WCE is scheduled to open to traffic in the first quarter of 2027, marking a key milestone in the highway's development and improving west coast connectivity. CIMB Securities said construction progress has accelerated following a breakthrough in land acquisition disputes with 19 residents in Kampung Jawa, Klang, in December 2025.

items.title: Chemical sector plays quiet but critical role in Malaysia's economic growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's electronics and semiconductor industries often dominate discussions on the country's economic growth, but industry players say the chemical sector plays a less visible yet equally important role in generating domestic economic value. The sector also supports high-skilled jobs and strengthens industrial resilience. By comparison, the chemical sector retains about 61 per cent of its value within Malaysia, underscoring its deeper integration into the domestic economy.

items.title: Price Is What You Pay, Value Is What You Get
items.summary: Legendary investor Warren Buffett once said, "Price is what you pay; value is what you get." Value, however, is deeper and often invisible at first glance. Two products may carry the same price but provide completely different levels of value.

items.title: The keys to Petronas
items.summary: National oil companies do not exist to service the present. The question now confronting Malaysia, as two significant transactions with Saudi Aramco have made visible, is whether we are managing Petronas as a vehicle for long-term wealth creation or as a short-term source of petrodollars. Petronas was designed, through the Petroleum Development Act of 1974, to be Malaysia's most consequential institutional answer to the problem of time.

items.title: Mounting losses put Kuala Juru's 80-year-old cockle industry in peril
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: A pollution crisis blamed for widespread cockle deaths is threatening the future of Kuala Juru's 80-year-old cockle industry, with fishermen warning that mounting losses could force many breeders out of business. Wahid said fishermen suspected the tributary, known locally as Sungai Derhaka, was carrying industrial pollutants into Sungai Juru, less than a kilometre from their breeding grounds. He said the pollution had devastated cockle harvests, with mortality rates reaching unprecedented levels.

items.title: Pentagon awards Microsoft US$9.7bil deal in bid to cut costs, end licence sprawl
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The Pentagon on Wednesday announced a five-year, US$9.69 billion agreement to consolidate Microsoft and other enterprise software licences scattered across the military services, the intelligence community, and the US Coast Guard into a single contract vehicle, officials said. The cost-cutting effort hands Microsoft a guaranteed enterprise-wide foothold across the US armed forces while squeezing out duplicative spending that officials said had quietly ballooned across years of fragmented, go-it-alone procurement. The deal, called the Core Enterprise Technology Agreement, is not new spending because baskets of Pentagon software contracts came up for renewal simultaneously.

items.title: Low oxygen levels in Sungai Juru trigger concern over cockle, fish deaths
items.summary: State Environment Committee chairman Datuk Seri S. Sundarajoo said following complaints from fishermen in Kuala Juru, the Department of Environment (DoE) had conducted an investigation and uncovered alarming readings at several locations along the river. He said the DoE received a complaint from Kuala Juru fishermen's representative Wahid Othman on May 12. DoE officers conducted field inspections the following day and carried out water quality tests at six points, including around Sungai Derhaka, which flowed through the Prai Free Industrial Zone.

items.title: Dead cockles spark alarm in Juru [WATCH]
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: Sungai Juru may be in danger of slipping into another environmental disaster. They also said they saw black spots on the water that emitted a strong stench. The fishermen believe that this was due to industrial and aquaculture pollution, an environmental group said.

items.title: Immigration dismissals point to deeper integrity vulnerabilities, says expert
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: enforcement, improved intelligence gathering and greater willingness by management to act against corrupt officers. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said offences include breaches of standard operating procedures, abuse of authority, absenteeism, drugs and smuggling prohibited items into immigration depots. Once citizens begin perceiving immigration officers as corrupt, compromised or selective in enforcement, confidence in border security, immigration control and national security weakens significantly."

items.title: Crash wasn’t your fault? BNM says knock‑for‑knock keeps NCD safe, repairs rolling without insurer drama
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — When a motor accident occurs, many drivers assume they must pursue the other party’s insurer for compensation — a process that can become drawn out while liability and fault are determined. Per BNM’s Claims Settlement Practices Policy Document, general insurers and takaful operators (GITO) must offer policyholders the option to submit an OD-KfK claim to expedite claims processing upon receiving accident notification. General insurers and takaful operators (GITO) must offer policyholders the option to submit an OD-KfK claim to expedite claims processing upon receiving accident notification.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh calls for private sector collaboration, upgraded facilities as KL’s concert boom nears RM1.7b
items.summary: According to Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh, this is due to the overwhelming demand for Perbadanan Stadium Malaysia’s (PBM) venues for concerts and live events all year round. Currently, the venues managed by PBM include the TM National Stadium (previously known as Bukit Jalil National Stadium) and the Unifi Arena Bukit Jalil (previously known as Axiata Arena). PBM has also recently reopened the National Hockey Stadium in Bukit Jalil for concerts and live events to cater to this demand.

items.title: Armed Forces retiree killed after motorcycle collides with BMW in Kampung Pandan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — An Armed Forces retiree was killed when the motorcycle he was riding was involved in a collision with a car along Jalan Kampung Pandan here on Monday. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said that the man, 45, was riding his Yamaha 135LC in the left lane and hit the right side of a BMW that came out of a junction from the left side of the road. The car driver, who was arrested yesterday to facilitate investigation under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, tested negative for alcohol and drugs.

items.title: MOF: RON97 drops to RM4.65, RON95 down to RM3.92, diesel at RM4.87
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Malaysians will see lower fuel prices this week, with the Ministry of Finance confirming retail adjustments effective May 28 to June 3. RON97 petrol falls 20 sen to RM4.65 per litre, unsubsidised RON95 slips 15 sen to RM3.92, while diesel in Peninsular Malaysia drops 10 sen to RM4.87. Subsidised fuel prices remain unchanged.

items.title: Search underway for 10-year-old boy feared drowned in Sungai Selisek
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — A 10-year-old boy is feared to have drowned after he was believed to have been swept away by current in Sungai Nilam, Sungai Selisek, Hulu Selangor, this afternoon. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant operations director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said his department received an emergency call at 1.40 pm and a team of personnel from Kuala Kubu Baharu Fire and Rescue Station was dispatched to the incident location which is about 40 kilometres away. He said the first engine arrived at the location at 2.35 pm with a strength of five personnel along with a Fire Rescue Tender (FRT), a Hilux and a boat.

items.title: Melaka braces for influx of 300,000 vehicles as festive, school break crowds grow
items.summary: MELAKA, May 27 (Bernama) -- Melaka is bracing for the influx of more than 300,000 vehicles during the Aidiladha holidays and school break, which is set to give the state a significant boost in tourist arrivals. State Senior Exco for Housing, Local Government, Climate Change and Disaster Management, Datuk Rais Yasin, said local authorities are prepared to ensure visitor safety, cleanliness and comfort are at the highest levels. The state government, through local authorities, remains on standby for any eventuality, including disasters, said Rais.

items.title: Teacher remanded over alleged sexual misconduct involving student in Besut
items.summary: JERTIH, May 27 — A male teacher has been remanded for two days starting today to assist in the investigation of a sexual misconduct case involving a secondary school student in Besut. Previously, Besut district police chief Superintendent Mohd Rozaime Ab Rahman said that the teacher, from a secondary school in the Besut district, was arrested around 3 pm yesterday following a police report made by two students. The issue of a male teacher allegedly molesting a male student in Besut went viral after a Facebook account owner posted about it a few days ago.

items.title: Heavy rain and strong winds alert: Sabah, Sarawak brace for severe thunderstorms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — Malaysia’s Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for several areas across East Malaysia, cautioning residents to expect heavy rain, strong winds, and lightning until 6:00 pm today. The warning, released at 3:35 pm, covers a wide section of Sarawak, including the districts of Kuching, Serian, Samarahan, Kapit (Song), Miri (Miri and Marudi), and Limbang. In Sabah, the alert applies to the Pedalaman region — specifically Sipitang, Kuala Penyu, and Beaufort — as well as Ranau on the West Coast, Tawau, and parts of Sandakan, encompassing Telupid, Kinabatangan, Beluran, and Sandakan town.

items.title: Immigration chief: 77 officers dismissed since 2022 amid crackdown on integrity breaches
items.summary: KAJANG, May 27 — A total of 77 Malaysian Immigration Department officers have been sacked from 2022 to date, out of 994 disciplinary cases resolved by the agency. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said that for this year alone, 23 disciplinary cases have been settled, with eight officers receiving punitive measures in an effort to strengthen integrity and curb misconduct within the department. Zakaria said misconduct included breaches of standard operating procedures, approving matters beyond authority, manipulating counter settings, absenteeism, drug abuse, and smuggling contraband to detainees at depots.

items.title: Baby girl dies after being found face down in pail of soapy water in Seremban
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 27 — Police have confirmed that an 11-month-old baby girl died yesterday after she was found face down in a pail of soapy water in a house backyard at Jalan Sri Permata Ladang. Seremban police chief ACP Mohd Yatim Osman said the department received a report on the incident at 7.22 pm. He said the victim was taken to a nearby clinic before being referred to Hospital Tuanku Ja’afar, where a medical officer confirmed her death at 4.30 pm.

items.title: Woman killed, five injured in MPV-car crash near Kuala Krai petrol station
items.summary: KUALA KRAI, May 27 — A woman was killed while five others, including two boys, were injured in an accident involving a multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) and a car near a Caltex petrol station in Laloh here this morning. Kuala Krai Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) chief Nik Ahmad Afsham Nik Pa said the victim, Safinaz Razmi, 52, was pronounced dead at the scene by Ministry of Health (MOH) personnel. The road accident involved a Toyota Alphard MPV and a Perodua Bezza car.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim hosts royal family gathering for Hari Raya Aidiladha at Johor palace
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 27 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, shared a meal with family members and grandchildren in conjunction with the Hari Raya Aidiladha celebration at Istana Bukit Serene here today. According to a post on the Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar Facebook page, the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, and Che’ Puan Mahkota Khaleeda Johor, together with their children, were also in attendance. Also present were Tunku Panglima Johor Tunku Abdul Rahman Sultan Ibrahim, Tunku Putera Johor Tunku Abu Bakar Sultan Ibrahim, as well as Tunku Tun Aminah Sultan Ibrahim and her husband Datuk Dennis Muhammad Abdullah, accompanied by their children.

items.title: Pandan run? Tengku Zafrul says will contest in any seat PKR picks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 —Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz has declared his readiness to contest in any constituency, including Pandan, in the upcoming elections following the resignation of Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli. Earlier, PKR Vice-President Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari announced that Tengku Zafrul has been appointed as the party’s monitoring officer for the constituency. This appointment comes after the constituency was “adopted” by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim following Rafizi’s resignation.

items.title: New PJ hospital site finalised; Selangor MB pledges to expedite approvals for 500-bed facility
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — The site for the long-awaited Petaling Jaya public hospital has been officially confirmed, paving the way for a new 500-bed facility to serve the rapidly growing population of the Petaling district. Currently, residents in the area rely on Hospital Sungai Buloh, Hospital Shah Alam, and the University Malaya Medical Centre (UMMC). The new hospital is expected to provide much-needed relief for residents in Petaling Jaya, Subang Jaya, and Puchong.

items.title: Fahmi: PKR gains more than 5,000 new members without major campaigns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — PKR recorded more than 5,000 new members between April and this month, said PKR information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. Fahmi, who is Lembah Pantai MP, said 10 of the cows were managed at the main site of the Lembah Pantai Korban Perdana Perpaduan programme today. Fahmi said the programme was also attended by representatives from Unity Government component parties, including Lembah Pantai Amanah, UMNO and PKR.

items.title: Muslims nationwide mark Aidiladha with prayers, royal attendance across states
items.summary: In Selangor, the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, joined more than 15,000 worshippers in performing the Aidiladha prayers at Masjid Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah in Shah Alam. Also present were the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin and the Raja Muda of Selangor Tengku Amir Shah. In Kedah, the Sultan of Kedah Al-Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah performed the Aidiladha prayers at Masjid Zahir in Alor Setar together with more than 2,000 congregants, including Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor and state secretary Datuk Seri Norizan Khazali.

items.title: Hamzah Zainudin to launch new political party in mid-June, source reveals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — A new political party led by Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin is expected to be unveiled in the middle of next month, according to a source aligned with the former Bersatu deputy president. The source, who was also sacked from Bersatu, said preparations for the launch in Kuala Lumpur were “almost complete”, Free Malaysia Today reported. “Yes, Hamzah will announce his new party in mid-June.

items.title: Police officer and wife killed, four children injured in Pekan crash
items.summary: Pekan police chief Supt Mohd Zaidi Mat Zin said the victims were Inspector Syah Lokman Ludza Ramli, 50, who served at the Kuantan District Police Headquarters, and his wife Nurulfiza Ardiana Ambak, 46, both of whom were pronounced dead at the scene. He said the accident is believed to have occurred at about 3 am and involved a Proton Wira driven by the victim and a Honda City. “Initial investigations found that the Honda City, driven by a 26-year-old man, was travelling from Gelang Patah to Terengganu, while the Proton Wira driven by the victim was heading from Kuantan towards Jempol.

items.title: Malaysian man arrested in Bangkok airport over alleged attempt to smuggle 250 wild animals
items.summary: BANGKOK, May 27 — Thai authorities have arrested a Malaysian man at Suvarnabhumi Airport for allegedly attempting to smuggle more than 250 live wild animals to India, in the latest crackdown on transnational wildlife trafficking networks. According to Thai authorities, the suspect was due to travel from Bangkok’s Suvarnabhumi Airport to Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport in Kolkata, India. Authorities said the suspect has been charged under the Wildlife Preservation and Protection Act 2019, the Customs Act 2017, the Animal Epidemics Act 2015 and the Fisheries Royal Ordinance 2015.

items.title: Digital education key to Online Safety Act age verification rollout, says cyber group
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — The implementation of an age verification mechanism under the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA) must be supported by comprehensive digital education efforts to ensure users, especially young people, understand the risks and challenges in cyberspace. Malaysia Cyber Consumer Association (MCCA) president Siraj Jalil said the association supports the government’s move to introduce the mechanism to prevent underage children from owning social media accounts, but its implementation must be accompanied by broader explanations and engagement sessions. “We agree with the age verification mechanism because social media platforms have long set age guidelines, but there has never been a truly effective method to ensure that ages are not falsified.

items.title: Steven Sim says Malaysia’s diversity is key strength in attracting global investors
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 27 — Malaysia’s multicultural society and cultural understanding became key economic advantages in attracting global investors and expanding international business ties, said Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Development Minister Steven Sim. Sim said therefore, the country’s diversity should be viewed as a national strength rather than a weakness, particularly in an increasingly competitive global economy. He said that this multicultural advantage complemented the country’s political stability and strategic geographical position amid growing global economic uncertainty.

items.title: Malaysian Armed Forces expresses gratitude to TMJ for ‘unwavering support’ amid military training row
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — The Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) has expressed its “highest gratitude” to the Regent of Johor (TMJ), Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, for his public backing of military exercises and his commitment to the welfare of veterans. In a statement today, MAF said the regent’s support reflects a “strong strategic synergy” between the royal institution, the Johor state government, and the military. The military also highlighted the Regent’s assurance that Johor’s doors remain open to MAF veterans.

items.title: ‘I got the boot’: Subang MP Wong Chen claims abrupt removal from MDV after turnaround
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — Subang MP Wong Chen has revealed his abrupt removal as the non-executive chairman of Malaysia Debt Ventures (MDV), despite leading the government-linked company (GLC) back to profitability during his three-year tenure. The letter from the MoF, dated May 25, 2026, instructed that his chairmanship be terminated effective May 31. “MoF didn't even bother to address the letter to me.

items.title: Fahmi: Putrajaya to examine’s Suhakam’s Taiping Prison findings before taking action
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 27 — The government will scrutinise Suhakam’s findings on the Taiping Prison incident that left one inmate dead before deciding on further action, says Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. We are concerned about Suhakam’s findings. One detainee, Gan Chin Eng, died following the incident.
